title: Drug Dosage Forms
summary: أشكال الجرعات الدوائية تُقسَّم إلى أربع فئات رئيسية: صلبة (Solid) كالكبسولات والأقراص والحبوب، وسائلة (Liquid) كالمحاليل والمعلقات والمستحلبات، وشبه صلبة (Semi-solid) كالمراهم والكريمات والهلاميات والتحاميل، وأشكال أخرى كاللصقات الجلدية. يهدف استخدام أشكال متنوعة إلى تحسين الامتصاص، وإخفاء الطعم الكريه، وتمديد مدة التأثير (Sustained-release)، وتسهيل الإعطاء لمختلف فئات المرضى. الأقراص المعوية (Enteric-coated) مقاومة لعصارة المعدة وتُطلق الدواء في الأمعاء ذات التفاعل القلوي. الأشكال المستدامة الإطلاق (Sustained-release) لا يجوز سحقها لأنها مصممة للإطلاق التدريجي.

key_drugs.name: Phenobarbital elixir
key_drugs.note: مثال على Elixir - محضر كحولي عطري محلى

key_drugs.name: Cod liver oil emulsion
key_drugs.note: مثال على Emulsion - دهن مستحلب في ماء

key_drugs.name: Corticosteroids
key_drugs.note: تأتي بأشكال متعددة: أقراص، حقن، مراهم، بخاخات

mnemonics.title: أشكال الجرعات الرئيسية - SLSO
mnemonics.body:
S = Solid (صلبة): Capsules, Tablets, Pills, Powders, Lozenges
L = Liquid (سائلة): Solutions, Syrups, Suspensions, Emulsions, Elixirs, Drops
S = Semi-solid (شبه صلبة): Ointments, Creams, Gels, Suppositories
O = Other: Transdermal patches, Aerosols
تذكر: 'سِلَسو' = Solid, Liquid, Semi-solid, Other

mnemonics.title: قصة الكبسولة والقرص
mnemonics.body: الكبسولة (Capsule) = البيت الجيلاتيني: بيت ناعم يحمي محتوياته (مسحوق أو سائل) ويذوب في المعدة بسرعة أكبر من القرص قليلاً. القرص الصلب (Tablet) = الحجر المضغوط: يُضغط ويُشكَّل، أقوى من الكبسولة. الكبسولة المعوية (Enteric-coated) = المعطف المقاوم للمعدة: لا تُفتح إلا في الأمعاء القلوية

mnemonics.title: الفرق بين Suspension و Emulsion
mnemonics.body: تخيل كأسين: الأول يحتوي عصير برتقال بالعجوة (Suspension = جسيمات صلبة في سائل). الثاني يحتوي خلطة زيت وماء مع مادة مستحلبة (Emulsion = دهن في ماء). كلاهما يحتاج رجاً قبل الاستخدام!

mnemonics.title: المراهم والكريمات - W/O vs O/W
mnemonics.body:
Water-in-Oil (W/O) = الماء داخل الزيت = الدهن الثقيل الذي يبقى على الجلد = Ointment (مرهم دهني)
Oil-in-Water (O/W) = الزيت داخل الماء = الكريم الخفيف المائي = Cream (كريم أخف)
تذكر: المرهم أثقل وأكثر دهنية، الكريم أخف وأقل دهنية

mnemonics.title: لا تسحق SR - Sustained Release
mnemonics.body:
SR = Sustained Release = إطلاق مستدام = NO CRUSH
السبب: تحتوي على جسيمات دقيقة مغلفة بمواد تذوب بأوقات مختلفة (بعضها فوري، بعضها 2-3 ساعات) لضمان امتصاص طويل ومتواصل. سحقها يُطلق الجرعة كاملة فجأة = خطر جرعة زائدة

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a hard and a soft gelatin capsule?
flashcards.back:
Hard gelatin capsule: contains a powder
Soft gelatin capsule: contains liquids or semi-solid materials

flashcards.front: What is an enteric-coated capsule/tablet and what is its benefit?
flashcards.back: It is coated with a material that resists gastric juice and does not disintegrate until it reaches the alkaline intestinal secretions. It is used to protect drugs that are destroyed by stomach acid or that irritate the stomach.

flashcards.front: Why should sustained-release tablets not be crushed?
flashcards.back: Because they contain coated particles that release the drug gradually. Crushing releases the entire dose at once, which may cause an overdose.

flashcards.front: What are lozenges (troches)?
flashcards.back: Flat, round, or oblong tablets held in the mouth until they dissolve, producing a temporarily high drug concentration in the oral cavity.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a suspension and a solution?
flashcards.back:
Solution: one or more substances completely dissolved in water (a true solution)
Suspension: a finely divided drug suspended in a liquid (not fully dissolved, requires shaking)

flashcards.front: What is an emulsion? Give an example.
flashcards.back: A suspension of fats or oils in water with the help of an emulsifying agent. Example: Cod liver oil emulsion.

flashcards.front: What is an elixir? Give an example.
flashcards.back: An aromatic, sweetened, alcoholic preparation used as a flavored vehicle or as an active medicinal agent. Example: Phenobarbital elixir.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between an ointment and a cream in terms of formulation?
flashcards.back:
Ointment: W/O (water-in-oil) formulation - more greasy and heavier
Cream: O/W (oil-in-water) formulation - lighter and absorbed faster

flashcards.front: What are the four types of injectable drug containers?
flashcards.back:
1. Ampoules (single dose)
2. Vials (multi-dose with a rubber stopper)
3. Bottles
4. Disposable syringes (single-use syringes)

flashcards.front: What is a transdermal therapeutic system? Give an example.
flashcards.back: A therapeutic system applied to the skin to achieve systemic effects. Example: Nitroglycerin patch for the treatment of angina pectoris.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a rectal suppository and a vaginal insert?
flashcards.back:
Rectal suppository: administered via the rectum
Vaginal inserts: include tablets and pessaries and are administered via the vagina

flashcards.front: What are the benefits of solid dosage forms compared with powders?
flashcards.back: They divide the drug into defined doses, avoid the inconvenience of preparing a powder, and mask the unpleasant taste of drugs.

flashcards.front: What are the types of medicinal sprays?
flashcards.back:
1. Nasal spray
2. Oral spray
3. Local spray

flashcards.front: What are dusting powders?
flashcards.back: Fine powders for external (topical) use that are applied to the skin.

flashcards.front: Why are drops used for children?
flashcards.back: Because children often cannot swallow tablets, and oral drops are easy to administer in small, precise doses.

flashcards.front: What are pills, and are they still common?
flashcards.back: Medicinal mixtures shaped into spheres or ovals for swallowing. They have become rare and have largely been replaced by capsules and tablets.

flashcards.front: What are gels among the semi-solid dosage forms?
flashcards.back: Semisolid forms containing a high proportion of water, transparent or semi-transparent, and easily absorbed.

flashcards.front: What is an enteric-coated capsule?
flashcards.back: A capsule/tablet coated with a material that resists gastric juice so it does not disintegrate until it reaches the alkaline intestine.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a hard and a soft capsule?
flashcards.back: The hard (hard gelatine) one contains a powder; the soft one contains liquids.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of sustained-release dosage forms?
flashcards.back: Gradual, continuous release leading to a longer duration of action; they must not be crushed.

flashcards.front: What are lozenges (troches)?
flashcards.back: Tablets held in the mouth until they dissolve to produce a high local concentration in the oral cavity.

flashcards.front: Rank the dissolution/absorption rate of oral dosage forms.
flashcards.back: Solution > Suspension > Capsule > Tablet.

flashcards.front: Why are tablets sometimes used instead of powders?
flashcards.back: To provide defined doses, to mask an unpalatable taste, and for ease of use.

flashcards.front: What are the liquid dosage forms of drugs?
flashcards.back: Solutions, suspensions, emulsions, and syrups.

flashcards.front: What are dusting powders?
flashcards.back: Fine powders for external use on the skin.

mcqs.q: Which type of capsule contains liquid medicament?
mcqs.explanation: Soft gelatin capsules تحتوي على سوائل أو مواد شبه صلبة، بينما Hard gelatin capsules تحتوي على مساحيق.

mcqs.q: Why should sustained-release tablets NOT be crushed?
options: Crushing causes rapid release of the entire dose at once
mcqs.explanation: الأقراص SR تحتوي جسيمات مغلفة تُطلق الدواء تدريجياً. السحق يُطلق الجرعة كاملة فجأة مما يخلق خطر الجرعة الزائدة.

options: A drug dissolved completely in water
Finely divided drug particles suspended in a liquid vehicle
A suspension of fats or oils in water using an emulsifying agent
mcqs.explanation: Emulsion = تعليق الدهون أو الزيوت في الماء بمساعدة عامل مستحلب. مثال: Cod liver oil emulsion.

mcqs.q: Ointments are classified as which type of emulsion?
mcqs.explanation: Ointments = W/O (Water-in-Oil) تركيبة دهنية أثقل. أما Creams فهي O/W (Oil-in-Water) أخف.

mcqs.q: Enteric-coated capsules are designed to dissolve in:
mcqs.explanation: Enteric-coated = مغلفة بمادة تقاوم حمض المعدة ولا تتفكك إلا في الإفرازات القلوية للأمعاء.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a semi-solid dosage form?
mcqs.explanation: Suppository = تحميلة = شكل شبه صلب. Elixir و Suspension = سائل. Lozenge = صلب.

mcqs.q: Lozenges produce their effect primarily by:
options: Creating temporarily high drug concentration in the oral cavity
mcqs.explanation: Lozenges تذوب في الفم وتُركز الدواء مؤقتاً في التجويف الفموي لتأثير موضعي.

mcqs.q: Which is an example of a Transdermal therapeutic system?
mcqs.explanation: Nitroglycerin patch = لصقة جلدية لعلاج الذبحة الصدرية، مثال كلاسيكي على Transdermal therapeutic system.

mcqs.q: A vial differs from an ampoule in that it:
options: Is used only for oral medications
Has a rubber-capped stopper allowing multiple doses
Cannot be used for injectable medications
mcqs.explanation: Vial = حاوية متعددة الجرعات بسدادة مطاطية. Ampoule = جرعة واحدة مغلقة.

mcqs.q: Which liquid dosage form is described as 'aromatic, sweetened, alcoholic preparations'?
mcqs.explanation: Elixir = محضر كحولي عطري محلى. Syrup = سكري غير كحولي. Solution = محلول مائي.

mcqs.q: الشكل الذي يُمنع سحقه لأنه يحرر الدواء تدريجياً:
mcqs.explanation: سحق ممتد التحرر يحرر الجرعة كاملة دفعة واحدة → سُمّية.
يقاوم حمض المعدة ويتفكك في الأمعاء القلوية.
